name: Tara
description: Tara, also known as Teamhair na Ríogh (Hill of the Kings), is an ancient ceremonial and burial site in County Meath, Ireland. It holds immense significance in Irish mythology and history, traditionally regarded as the seat of the High Kings of Ireland. For centuries, Tara was the political and spiritual center of the island, witnessing the inauguration of kings and the gathering of druids and bards. The site is not a single structure but rather a complex of earthworks, including ringforts, mounds, and standing stones, each imbued with its own mythic narrative. The Lia Fáil (Stone of Destiny), located on the Mound of Inauguration, was said to roar when a rightful king placed his foot upon it. Tara is associated with numerous figures from Irish mythology, including the Tuatha Dé Danann, who are said to have ruled from there. Legends speak of feasts, assemblies, and divine interventions occurring on its sacred grounds. Even after its political importance waned, Tara remained a powerful symbol of Irish identity and sovereignty, inspiring poets, artists, and revolutionaries throughout history. The hill continues to evoke a sense of awe and connection to the ancient past, representing the heart of Irish heritage and the enduring power of its mythology. The site features prominent structures like the Rath of the Synods, the Royal Seat, and the Teach Miodhchuarta (Banqueting Hall). Archaeological excavations have revealed evidence of human activity dating back to the Neolithic period, suggesting that Tara was a significant site long before the rise of the High Kings.
subtitle: Seat of the High Kings of Ireland

keyMyths.title: The Stone of Destiny
keyMyths.description: The Lia Fáil, or Stone of Destiny, was said to roar when a true king stood upon it, signifying his rightful claim to the throne. This event was a crucial part of the inauguration ceremony at Tara.

keyMyths.title: The High Kings of Tara
keyMyths.description: Tara was the seat of the High Kings of Ireland, who ruled the island from this sacred location. These kings were believed to possess both political and spiritual authority, ensuring prosperity for the land.

sources.author: Geoffrey Keating
sources.description: A history of Ireland from earliest times to the Norman invasion.
sources.text: A history of Ireland from earliest times to the Norman invasion.

sources.author: John O'Donovan
